full_response:
Step-by-step derivation  

1.  Label the elements.  
   For 3 × 3 matrices we denote
        M = [M_{ij}], N = [N_{ij}], with i,j = 1,2,3 .

2.  Definition of matrix multiplication.  
   The element in the i-th row and j-th column of the product MN is obtained by
   taking the dot product of the i-th row of M with the j-th column of N.  
   Algebraically this is a sum over the common (dummy) index k that runs from 1 to 3.

3.  Write the formula with Σ notation:  
        (MN)_{ij} = Σ_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik}\,N_{kj}, with i,j = 1,2,3 .

4.  In more compact index notation one simply writes  
        (MN)_{ij} = Σ_{k} M_{ik} N_{kj} ,

   where the limits of the sum (k = 1…3) are understood for 3×3 matrices.

That Σ expression is the requested result; the row vector v and column vector u would be multiplied in exactly the same way:

  (vM)_j = Σ_{k} v_k M_{kj},  (Mu)_i = Σ_{k} M_{ik} u_k.

The essential answer, however, is the boxed formula for [M]·[N].

\boxed{(MN)_{ij} = \displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik}\,N_{kj}\qquad(i,j = 1,2,3)}

Confidence: 92%
